# LayerHierarchy

**Class**

An object that holds a reference to layers rendered in another process’s view.

## Declaration

```swift
@MainActor class LayerHierarchy
```

## Overview

In your browser’s rendering extension, create a `LayerHierarchy` and set its [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/LayerHierarchy/layer] to a [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/QuartzCore/CALayer] that renders content to host in a view in the browser app. Get the layer hierarchy’s [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/LayerHierarchy/handle], and send it to the browser app, where you add the handle to a [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/LayerHierarchyHostingView].

Use [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/LayerHierarchyHostingTransactionCoordinator] to synchronize updates in the view and the layer. For more information, see [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/hosting-browser-view-layers-in-the-rendering-extension].

## Creating and invalidating a layer hierarchy

- **init()**: Initializes a layer hierarchy.
- **invalidate()**: Invalidates a layer hierarchy.

## Setting the layer

- **layer**: The layer represented by this layer hierarchy.

## Getting a handle

- **handle**: A reference to the layer hierarchy that you add to the hosting view.

## Layer hosting

- **Hosting browser view layers in the rendering extension**: Coordinate view-hierarchy and layer-hierarchy changes between processes.
- **LayerHierarchyHostingView**: A view that hosts a layer hierarchy you manage in another process.
- **LayerHierarchyHostingTransactionCoordinator**: Synchronizes updates to views and layers in different processes.
- **LayerHierarchyHandle**: A reference to a layer hierarchy that you share between processes.

## Inherits From

- NSObject

## Conforms To

- CVarArg
- CustomDebugStringConvertible
- CustomStringConvertible
- Equatable
- Hashable
- NSObjectProtocol
- Sendable
